description: Goal: once defmacro has been cut off from the runtime root pipeline and ordinary function-body expansion has been moved behind function demand, reduce the quoted-source/source-surface spine to one coherent scope-tier model: quoted roots are the only source authority, item-level expansion is root->root over one heap, publication is one transaction, and compiler2 type/spec/extern fragments travel as preserved token payloads instead of being reconstructed or re-lexed. Signal: ordinary source forms, item-macro-expanded source fragments, and Fz.Compiler.define all feed the same raw publication engine; item-level expansion is separated from publication; compiler2 no longer re-renders quoted type fragments back into tokens; the relevant fz2 source-surface fixtures become tractable without bespoke paths. Strategy: after function staging lands, collapse duplicate raw publication logic, then split item/source expansion from publication, then standardize fragment transport, then narrow quote/unquote lowering, and finally remove retired code paths and restate the design in docs and telemetry.
title: Compiler2 source spine cleanup: unify publication, expansion, and fragment transport
